DESCRIPTION: A diagram illustrating the application of various nanomaterials such as graphene, boron nitride, Xene, TMDs, and nanotubes in tribology to enhance energy efficiency, featuring a tribological contact and emphasizing 2D nanocomposites.

URL: https://xpic.x-mol.com/20231002/10.1016_j.cis.2023.103004.jpg

enriched_points.expanded_content: Advancements in MXene and 2D nanomaterials as lubricant additives significantly reduce friction and wear, enhancing energy efficiency in tribological applications.

enriched_points.point_title: Integration of AI in Tribological Research
enriched_points.expanded_content: AI technologies can optimize tribological processes by predicting material behaviors, enhancing lubricant formulations, and improving system performance through data analysis.
Interdisciplinary partnerships among academia, automotive, and manufacturing sectors foster innovation in tribology, leading to sustainable energy-efficient solutions and improved product longevity.
